content:
# IVX HOLDINGS — 12 AI SENIOR DEVELOPER AUDIT AND ROLE MAP

## OBJECTIVE

Audit all 12 existing IVX AI agents and determine whether each one is actually operating at a senior developer level.

Do not rename them as senior developers without evidence.

This task must produce:

1. A complete audit of all 12 AI agents
2. A seniority score for each AI
3. A permanent developer role for each AI
4. A clear ownership map
5. Proof of real capabilities
6. A final IVX engineering organization chart
7. A live dashboard showing what each AI is doing

This is an audit and implementation task, not a narrative response.

---

# PHASE 1 — DISCOVER THE EXISTING 12 AI AGENTS

Identify every currently configured AI agent in IVX Holdings.

For each AI, return:

* AI number
* Current name
* Current system prompt
* Current responsibility
* Current tools
* Repository access
* Database access
* Deployment access
* Testing access
* Files or modules owned
* Active tasks
* Last completed task
* Last commit SHA
* Last deployment ID
* Last verified production result
* Current blocker
* Whether the AI can execute code or only provide narrative

Do not assume capabilities.

Inspect the real configuration, code, prompts, tools, logs, database records and execution history.

---

# PHASE 2 — SENIOR DEVELOPER CAPABILITY TEST

Test every AI independently using the same senior developer scorecard.

Each AI must be tested on the following 20 capabilities:

1. Reads and understands the existing codebase
2. Locates the correct files before editing
3. Creates a technical plan
4. Identifies dependencies
5. Writes production-quality code
6. Avoids placeholder code
7. Avoids fake data
8. Avoids duplicate implementations
9. Creates database migrations correctly
10. Understands authentication and authorization
11. Handles errors correctly
12. Adds structured logs and trace IDs
13. Writes automated tests
14. Runs tests before completion
15. Fixes failed tests
16. Uses Git branches and commits correctly
17. Deploys through the approved pipeline
18. Verifies production after deployment
19. Provides rollback instructions
20. Provides proof instead of narrative

For every capability, assign:

* PASS
* PARTIAL
* FAIL
* NOT CONFIGURED

Every result must include evidence.

---

# PHASE 3 — PRACTICAL EXECUTION TEST

Assign each AI one controlled test task appropriate to its apparent specialty.

The test must be completed in a safe branch or staging environment.

Each test must require the AI to:

1. Inspect the existing implementation
2. Identify the root cause
3. Change the correct files
4. Add or update tests
5. Run tests
6. Commit the change
7. Produce the commit SHA
8. Deploy to staging where applicable
9. Verify the result
10. Produce screenshots, logs or API evidence

The AI fails the test if it:

* Only explains what should be done
* Produces a plan but no code
* Uses placeholders
* Fabricates proof
* Claims deployment without a deployment ID
* Claims testing without test output
* Asks for credentials without first verifying missing, expired, revoked or invalid credentials
* Changes unrelated modules
* Breaks an existing feature
* Cannot identify the files it changed

---

# PHASE 4 — SENIORITY CLASSIFICATION

Classify each AI using this scoring system.

## SENIOR DEVELOPER

Requirements:

* Score of 85% or higher
* Can work independently
* Produces complete code
* Writes tests
* Fixes failures
* Understands architecture
* Handles production risks
* Provides real evidence
* Does not rely on narrative-only answers

## MID-LEVEL DEVELOPER

Requirements:

* Score between 65% and 84%
* Can complete scoped work
* Needs architecture or review support
* May miss testing, security or deployment details

## JUNIOR DEVELOPER

Requirements:

* Score between 40% and 64%
* Requires close supervision
* Completes only small tasks
* Frequently produces incomplete changes

## NOT A DEVELOPER AGENT

Requirements:

* Score below 40%
* Cannot modify or test real code
* Only provides recommendations or narrative
* Has no verified execution ability

Do not label any AI as senior based only on its name or prompt.

---

# PHASE 5 — FINAL 12-AI DEVELOPER ROLE MAP

After the audit, assign these permanent roles based on demonstrated capability.

## AI 1 — Chief Software Architect

Owns:

* System architecture
* Technical standards
* Module boundaries
* Architecture reviews
* Cross-team technical decisions

## AI 2 — React Native Mobile Developer

Owns:

* Android
* iOS
* Expo
* Navigation
* Mobile performance
* APK and AAB readiness

## AI 3 — Web Frontend Developer

Owns:

* Landing page
* Web application
* Owner portal
* Responsive layouts
* Browser compatibility

## AI 4 — Backend API Developer

Owns:

* API routes
* Business logic
* Server middleware
* Error handling
* Health endpoints
* Rate limiting

## AI 5 — Database and Supabase Developer

Owns:

* Database schema
* Migrations
* Row-level security
* Realtime
* Backups
* Query performance

## AI 6 — Authentication and Security Developer

Owns:

* Owner login
* Member login
* Roles and permissions
* Password recovery
* Session security
* Secret validation
* Security reviews

## AI 7 — Chat and Realtime Developer

Owns:

* Direct messaging
* Chat rooms
* Message history
* Message ordering
* Duplicate prevention
* Realtime updates
* Read receipts

## AI 8 — Media and Reels Developer

Owns:

* Images
* Videos
* Reels
* Compression
* Thumbnails
* Upload reliability
* Playback performance
* CDN delivery

## AI 9 — DevOps and Cloud Developer

Owns:

* GitHub
* CI/CD
* Render
* Vercel
* AWS
* Expo EAS
* Production monitoring
* Rollbacks

## AI 10 — QA Automation Developer

Owns:

* Unit tests
* Integration tests
* End-to-end tests
* Regression testing
* Device testing
* Release validation

## AI 11 — Performance and Reliability Developer

Owns:

* Application performance
* Load testing
* Database performance
* Caching
* Monitoring
* Scaling
* Reliability engineering

## AI 12 — Code Review, Integration and Evidence Developer

Owns:

* Pull-request reviews
* Merge control
* Release evidence
* Commit verification
* Deployment verification
* Production proof
* Audit reporting

If an AI does not meet the requirements for its assigned role, report the gap and provide the exact configuration, prompt, tools and permissions required to upgrade it.

---

# PHASE 6 — AI OWNERSHIP RULES

Each AI must have exclusive primary ownership of its assigned area.

The system must prevent:

* Multiple agents editing the same files simultaneously
* Duplicate features
* Duplicate APIs
* Duplicate database tables
* Conflicting migrations
* Unreviewed production deployments
* Self-approval of critical changes
* Narrative-only completion

Every task must follow this flow:

1. Architect or Integration AI approves technical scope
2. Assigned developer AI implements
3. Security or Database AI reviews where applicable
4. QA AI tests
5. DevOps AI deploys
6. Evidence AI verifies production

No agent may mark its own task fully complete without independent verification.

---

# PHASE 7 — SHARED AI TASK LEDGER

Create one shared ledger for all 12 AI agents.

Each task must contain:

* Task ID
* Task title
* Module
* Assigned AI
* Reviewing AI
* Priority
* Status
* Start time
* Last activity time
* Files changed
* Database migrations
* API routes changed
* Test command
* Test result
* Commit SHA
* Pull request
* Deployment ID
* Production URL
* Verification evidence
* Blocker
* Remaining work

Allowed statuses:

* NOT STARTED
* ANALYZING
* IN PROGRESS
* CODE COMPLETE
* REVIEW REQUIRED
* TEST FAILED
* TEST PASSED
* DEPLOYMENT FAILED
* DEPLOYED
* PRODUCTION VERIFIED
* BLOCKED
* REJECTED

Only PRODUCTION VERIFIED counts as completed.

---

# PHASE 8 — OWNER DASHBOARD

Create an Owner-only dashboard named:

## IVX AI ENGINEERING COMMAND CENTER

Display all 12 AI agents.

For each AI show:

* AI number
* Name
* Assigned developer role
* Seniority level
* Capability score
* Current task
* Current status
* Progress percentage
* Last action
* Last file changed
* Last commit SHA
* Last deployment ID
* Last test result
* Current blocker
* Tasks completed today
* Tasks failed today
* Production-verified tasks
* Time since last activity

Add filters for:

* AI agent
* Role
* Status
* Module
* Date
* Completed
* Failed
* Blocked
* Deployed
* Production verified

Add drill-down pages showing the full evidence for every task.

---

# PHASE 9 — REQUIRED FINAL REPORT

Return a complete report using this exact structure.

## EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

* Total AI agents discovered
* Number classified as senior
* Number classified as mid-level
* Number classified as junior
* Number classified as non-developer
* Number with real repository execution
* Number with deployment capability
* Number with verified production evidence
* Critical gaps
* Recommended changes

## AI AUDIT TABLE

For each AI include:

| AI | Current Name | Current Capability | Score | Seniority | Assigned Role | Main Gap | Evidence |
| -- | ------------ | -----------------: | ----: | --------- | ------------- | -------- | -------- |

## TOOL AND ACCESS TABLE

For each AI include:

| AI | GitHub | Database | Render | Vercel | AWS | Expo | Test Runner | Production Access |
| -- | ------ | -------- | ------ | ------ | --- | ---- | ----------- | ----------------- |

## PRACTICAL TEST RESULTS

For each AI include:

* Test task
* Files changed
* Tests executed
* Test result
* Commit SHA
* Deployment ID
* Production verification
* Final result

## FINAL ROLE MAP

Show the final assignment of all 12 AI agents.

## REMEDIATION PLAN

For every AI below senior level, include:

* Missing capabilities
* Prompt changes
* Tool changes
* Permission changes
* Required tests
* Required training or guardrails
* Estimated implementation complexity

Do not use fabricated completion percentages.

---

# REQUIRED PROOF

The final audit must include real evidence where available:

* AI configuration locations
* Agent prompt locations
* Tool registrations
* Repository files
* Branch names
* Commit SHAs
* Pull requests
* Test command output
* Build output
* Render deployment IDs
* Expo build IDs
* Production URLs
* HTTP status results
* Screenshots
* Logs
* Trace IDs
* Database migration records

If evidence is unavailable, write:

EVIDENCE UNAVAILABLE — NOT VERIFIED

Do not replace missing evidence with assumptions.

---

# CREDENTIAL AND ACCESS RULE

Credentials and secrets are assumed to already exist in approved Variables or secret storage.

Do not ask the owner to resend credentials unless a real runtime verification proves that the credential is:

* Missing
* Deleted
* Expired
* Revoked
* Incorrect
* Returning 401
* Returning 403

First verify:

* Variable name
* Runtime binding
* Service environment
* Branch environment
* Build environment
* Permission scope
* Exact API response

Never print secret values.

---

# ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA

This task is complete only when:

1. All 12 AI agents are discovered
2. All 12 are practically tested
3. Each AI receives a real score
4. Each AI receives a seniority classification
5. Each AI receives one permanent developer role
6. Role conflicts are removed
7. The shared task ledger exists
8. The owner dashboard exists
9. Evidence is attached to every verified claim
10. The final report is delivered
11. Changes are committed
12. Deployment is verified
13. Production proof is provided

Do not answer only with recommendations.

Perform the audit, implement the role map, create the dashboard and return evidence.
